titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/246

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=221 status=live modified=2017-03-30 15:51:18.726468+00 | translation moa=220 status=live modified=2017-03-30 15:51:37.140095+00

div.p:
. W . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus Totius Terre sue clericis ⁊ laicis 
Salutem. Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri me concessisse ⁊ hac Carta mea confirmasse .
donacionem quam Willelmus de Veteri Ponte fecit deo ⁊ Sancte Cruci de Edeneb’ . ⁊ Cano
nicis ibidem deo seruientibus de terra de Okelfas’ . per rectas diuisas suas ⁊ cum
omnibus iustis pertinenciis suis . Tenend’ . Sibi . in liberam . ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosi
nam . cum omnibus ad predictam Terram iuste pertinentibus . Ita libere . ⁊ Quiete plenarie ⁊ ho
norifice  Sicut Carta predicti Willelmi de Veteri Ponte eis inde facta Testatur .
Saluo seruicio meo de terra illa ⁊ in auxiliis ⁊ in aliis de heredibus prefati Willelmi.
Teste . Walter . Capellano meo . Ph’ . de Valon’ . Camerario meo . Willelmo Cum’ . Willelmo de Bosch’ .
DD . de Haia . Willelmo de Morau’ . Ric’ Reuel’ . Henrico Reuel. Apud Forfar . quarto . die Nouembris.
Datum per manum Willelmi de Bosch’ . tempore Flor’ . Cancellarii . anno ab incarnatio . domini m.cciii

William by the grace of God King of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, the donation which William de Vieuxpont made to God and to Holy Rood at Edinburgh and to the Canons there serving God, of the land of Ogilface, by its correct bounds and with all its just pertinents. For them to hold in free and pure and perpetual alms, with all things justly pertaining to the aforesaid land, so freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid William de Vieuxpont made for them thereupon bears witness. Reserving my service from that land both in aids and in other things from the heirs of the aforementioned William. Witness, Walter my chaplain, Philip de Valognes my chamberlain, William Comyn, William del Bois, David de la Haye, William of Moray, Richard Revel, Henry Revel. At Forfar on the fourth day of November. Given by the hand of William del Bois, in the time of Florence the chancellor, in the year of the Incarnation of the Lord, 1203.
